# UC-19.1.39 · Proxmox Ceph Cluster HEALTH_WARN and OSD Flap Detection

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Reliability &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the shared storage system your virtual machines use together on this kind of server farm. If one storage helper drops out, starts challenging others, or the group cannot agree, things slow and then can stop. We raise a hand when the first serious warning shows up, not only when everything has already ground to a halt.*

---

## Description

Fuses a compact JSON `ceph -s -f json` poll (hyperconverged PVE) with Ceph `syslog` lines to detect `HEALTH_WARN` / `HEALTH_ERR`, MON **quorum** loss, **OSD up/in** shortfall, **degraded** PG share (from `pgs_by_state` via controlled `rex` and `tonumber` summation for common `active+*` state names), and **flap** / **election** patterns (`marked itself down`, `wrongly marked me down`, `boot`, new **election** strings, `osd.N` in context) per host, then **rolls up** to one row per Ceph `fsid` with a four-tier `severity_bucket` and operator-facing `active_health_checks` and `flap_score`.

## Value

A `HEALTH_WARN` page is the **cheapest** operational signal you can get from Ceph: the cluster is still **serving** I/O, placement groups are mostly **active+clean**, and you can often fix a bad disk, a flapping 10G link, or a stuck OSD before a second fault turns the state into `HEALTH_ERR` with **unfound** objects and **read-only** RBD child layers for every VM. Waiting for the guest OS to freeze or for customers to open tickets costs **outage** minutes and change-control chaos; a timely warn gives the storage and platform teams a **measured** window to **drain**, **reweight**, or **RMA** while backups and snapshots still have a consistent backing store. The business case is simple: a prevented **yellow-to-red** escalation avoids multi-hour recovery, data scrub backlog, and cross-team **Sev1** load when the only missing ingredient was a sixty-minute lead from telemetry you already had permission to collect.

## Implementation

Create `client.splunk_status`, deploy the `proxmox:ceph` scripted poller and `proxmox_ceph_collect` on one MON/MGR PVE per cluster (or one per `fsid` in multi-cluster sites), keep `Splunk_TA_nix` on Ceph log forwarders, save the `spl` as a 5- or 15-minute alert, throttle on `cluster_id`, and require **Pager** bodies to list `active_health_checks` and `quorum_loss_pct` for triage.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- A hyperconverged Proxmox VE + Ceph on-cluster deployment where the same PVE nodes run MON, MGR, and OSD members and are eligible to run `ceph -s` with a dedicated Ceph user. If your Ceph cluster is consumed externally by Proxmox in RBD-only client mode, the Ceph cluster’s own health monitoring is responsible for `HEALTH_WARN` — not the Proxmox poller. **This use case is for hyperconverged Proxmox where the Proxmox nodes ARE the MON / MGR / OSD hosts.**
- Splunk Universal Forwarder 9.0+ (or a supported HEC/aggregator) on the nodes that ship Ceph logs; search users need an allowed role for `index=virtualization` (or the index you standardize).
- The Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (`Splunk_TA_nix`, Splunkbase 833) on forwarders and indexers (or a site equivalent) for time zones and stable `host` + `program` for syslog. The canonical on-disk name uses an underscore (`Splunk_TA_nix`); the NIX add-on is the only Splunk-supported piece this UC depends on for syslog parsing and props.
- A custom app (example `proxmox_ceph_collect`) for the `ceph -s -f json` scripted input, deployed only on MON/MGR-capable nodes where the `ceph` CLI can reach the cluster with read-only creds. Do not embed `client.admin` in the poller.
- Ceph feature awareness: the JSON shape from `ceph -s -f json` moves between Octopus, Pacific, Quincy, Reef, and Squid; the SPL uses `coalesce()`-friendly first paths and the recovery `rex` lines in Step 5 if your release omits a scalar. Pin your Ceph version in the runbook and adjust `spath` only after you have a one-minute `| head` sample.

**Step 1 — Deploy the Ceph poller, privileges, and syslog collection**

(1) Create a read-only Ceph identity for the poller. Example: `client.splunk_status` with monitor + MGR read (no admin caps):

```text
ceph auth get-or-create client.splunk_status mon "allow r" mgr "allow r" \
  -o /etc/ceph/ceph.client.splunk_status.keyring
```

(2) Place `ceph_status_json.sh` in `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/proxmox_ceph_collect/bin/` (mode `0755`):

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
out="$(
  ceph -s -f json --name client.splunk_status \
    --keyring /etc/ceph/ceph.client.splunk_status.keyring --connect-timeout 5 2>/dev/null \
  | jq -c . 2>/dev/null
)" || exit 1
[ -n "$out" ] || exit 1
printf '%s\n' "$out"
```

If `jq` is missing, fall back in-place to `python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.dumps(json.load(sys.stdin),separators=(',',':')))"`, with the same non-zero contract on empty output.

(3) `local/inputs.conf` for the app:

```ini
[script://./bin/ceph_status_json.sh]
disabled = 0
interval = 60
index = virtualization
sourcetype = proxmox:ceph
source = ceph_status_json
```

(4) Forward Ceph file logs (OSD, MON, MGR) with `rsyslog` `imfile` into a UF, not by asking Splunk to read `/var/log/ceph` on indexers. Example lines (tune to your Ceph 17/18+ log file names on PVE 8/9):

```text
# imfile to local UF — Tag drives program= naming at the collector
input(type="imfile" File="/var/log/ceph/ceph-osd.*.log" Tag="ceph-osd:" readMode="0")
input(type="imfile" File="/var/log/ceph/ceph-mon.*.log" Tag="ceph-mon:" readMode="0")
input(type="imfile" File="/var/log/ceph/ceph-mgr.*.log" Tag="ceph-mgr:" readMode="0")
```

Use `host_segment` or a transforms rule so `host` is the PVE node FQDN or short name, and `sourcetype=syslog` on the index side.

**Step 2 — Build the multi-stream search and understand the extractions**

(1) Paste the `spl` field from this use case as the saved search body. The JSON line supplies `health.status`, `osdmap` counts, `monmap` / `quorum` length, `pgmap` totals, a reconstructed `degraded_pg_count` from the `pgs_by_state` array (via `rex` on the known `active+*` state names your cluster reports — extend the list in `local/` if you see rare combinations only in `| head` samples), and `health.checks` keys for `active_health_checks`. The syslog half contributes flap hits and `osd_id` for operator drilldown.

(2) `spath` on the JSON: after `| spath input=jraw` and `| spath` / `| spath "pgmap"`, the array form for PG distribution can also be read with `spath path=pgmap.pgs_by_state{}` in builds that expand `{state_name,count}`; if your Splunk build exposes `pgmap.pgs_by_state{}` as a multivalue, prefer `mvexpand` + `where match(state,"degraded|incomplete|undersized|recover|backfill|backfilling|remapped")` and `| stats sum(count)` in a clone search, then `| lookup` the result into the main cluster row.

(3) Smoke after the first good poll (must return a row; health string matches live Ceph):

```spl
index=virtualization sourcetype="proxmox:ceph" earliest=-5m
| spath
| spath "health"
| head 1
| table _time, "health.status", _indextime, _raw
```

(4) If the alert returns no rows, confirm `interval`, UF connectivity, and that `jraw` is not stderr-polluted. If the cluster is so small that a single `jq` hiccup leaves a stale `HEALTH_OK` line, add `_indextime` drift logic in a wrapper alert: `| where _indextime - _time < 300`.

**Step 3 — Cross-check the Proxmox web UI, `pvecm status`, and `ceph -s` text**

(1) Datacenter → Ceph in the Proxmox web UI: top-of-page health and OSD counts are the same signals the poller’s JSON is encoding.

(2) On shell: `ceph -s` (text) and `pvecm status` for coroside membership — these must agree directionally with `quorum` length and `fsid` in Splunk. If the UI is green but Splunk says `HEALTH_WARN`, read `_indextime` and `splunkd` on the poller: you may be looking at a stale one-line event.

(3) Version skew: the `ceph` major release on the node that runs the poller must be in-family with the MGR/MONs; a mismatched `ceph` client can print keys your `spath` does not name — capture `ceph version` in your CMDB and pin field lists.

**Step 4 — Operationalize dashboards, cadence, and pager text**

(1) Dashboard row A: one Single value for worst `severity_bucket` (last 1h) across clusters. Row B: Timechart of `num_up_osds` and `quorum_count` by `cluster_id`. Row C: Table of `active_health_checks` and `flap_score`. Optional sparkline: clone the syslog sub-branch only, `| timechart count by osd_id` for flap keywords.

(2) Critical alert: cron every 5 minutes, throttle on `cluster_id` for 20–30 minutes. Warning cadence: 15 minutes with the same suppress key. Body must echo `active_health_checks` and `quorum_loss_pct` so the on-call SRE can route to OSD/CRUSH work vs MON/NTP work vs MGR work without opening three bridges blind.

(3) Wire the Pager or webhook to include the Ceph `fsid` (`cluster_id`) and a one-line link to the Proxmox Ceph view for that site.

**Step 5 — Tuning and Ceph-only troubleshooting (gotchas)**

- Planned backfill after a disk or OSD add can hold degraded PGs for hours — pair this UC with a `maintenance_windows` lookup (keyed on `cluster_id` + change id) and suppress while `active+clean` is not yet restored but the change is authorized.

- `RECENT_CRASH` under `HEALTH_WARN` is often a stale crash archive, not a live fault — your storage team’s documented response is `ceph crash ls` and `ceph crash archive-all` after they accept the RCA, not a blind disk RMA from Splunk alone.

- `MON_CLOCK_SKEW`: this is an NTP problem first — every MON must be sub-second aligned before you tune pool parameters.

- `BLUEFS_SPILLOVER`: the fast BlueFS metadata device is too small for the working set — it is a slow-burn capacity and layout problem, not an instant I/O stop, but it will become one if ignored through several maintenance cycles.

- `ceph osd reweight` / out / in during change windows can create flap lines in logs that look like an outage; suppress with a change-calendar lookup or a time-bound macro.

- All MGRs down makes `ceph -s` and JSON nonsense or empty — add a pre-flight in the poller: if the JSON is missing `monmap` + a parsable `health` block, emit a one-line `POLL_FAILED` so Splunk does not show a fake `HEALTH_OK` from a partial buffer.

- `pgmap` older releases sometimes exposed `state_count` instead of a modern `pgs_by_state` array — the SPL’s `coalesce('pgmap.num_pgs', … )` and the `coalesce` note in the main search cover totals; for degraded, add a site-specific `rex` in `local/` for your object shape, or a short Python custom command if your security team allows it (not required for Quincy+).

- Rook, `cephadm` without Proxmox, or the Splunk Connect for Kubernetes path that lands Prometheus from `ceph-mgr` in a metrics index, is a different sourcetype and retention model than this UC — the `exclusions` field lists the boundary. Do not point this saved search at a metrics index without rewriting the early pipeline.

- Post-upgrade Ceph can introduce a one-time `HEALTH_WARN` from new health checks your dashboard author has not seen yet; capture `| fieldsummary` on a five-minute `proxmox:ceph` window after every major upgrade and add any new `state_name` strings to the degraded `rex` family (or a `local` `macros.conf` with your site’s PG-state vocabulary) so the `pg_degraded_pct` line does not read zero when the UI clearly shows a placement story.

Closing note (operator discipline)  
Keep a single Ceph owner in the paging policy who can read both JSON (truth for placement) and syslog (truth for liveness) in one sitting; split rota without a hand-off checklist is how duplicate bridges open when `active_health_checks` already names `OSD_FLAPPING` and the network team is still the default for every `ping`.

## SPL

```spl
index=virtualization (sourcetype="proxmox:ceph" OR (sourcetype=syslog (program=ceph OR program=ceph-mon OR program=ceph-osd OR program=ceph-mgr))) earliest=-1h@m
| eval is_json=if(sourcetype="proxmox:ceph",1,0)
| eval jraw=if(is_json=1, _raw, null())
| spath input=jraw
| spath
| spath "health"
| spath "osdmap"
| spath "pgmap"
| spath "monmap"
| rex field=jraw max_match=0 "\"fsid\"\\s*:\\s*\"(?<fsid_rex>[^\"]+)\""
| eval cluster_id=if(is_json=1, coalesce('monmap.fsid', fsid_rex, 'fsid'), null())
| eval health_status=if(is_json=1, coalesce('health.status', "POLL_FAILED"), null())
| eval num_osds=if(is_json=1, tonumber('osdmap.num_osds',10), null())
| eval num_up_osds=if(is_json=1, tonumber('osdmap.num_up_osds',10), null())
| eval num_in_osds=if(is_json=1, tonumber('osdmap.num_in_osds',10), null())
| eval num_mons=if(is_json=1, tonumber('monmap.num_mons',10), null())
| eval quorum_count=if(is_json=1, if(isnotnull('quorum') AND mvcount('quorum')>0, mvcount('quorum'), 0), null())
| eval total_pg=if(is_json=1, tonumber('pgmap.num_pgs',10), null())
| eval degraded_sum=0
| rex field=jraw max_match=0 "state_name\"\\s*:\\s*\"active\\+degraded\"\\s*,\\s*\"count\"\\s*:\\s*(?<d1>\\d+)"
| eval degraded_sum=if(is_json=1, degraded_sum+tonumber(d1,10), degraded_sum)
| rex field=jraw max_match=0 "state_name\"\\s*:\\s*\"active\\+incomplete\"\\s*,\\s*\"count\"\\s*:\\s*(?<d2>\\d+)"
| eval degraded_sum=if(is_json=1, degraded_sum+tonumber(d2,10), degraded_sum)
| rex field=jraw max_match=0 "state_name\"\\s*:\\s*\"active\\+undersized\"\\s*,\\s*\"count\"\\s*:\\s*(?<d3>\\d+)"
| eval degraded_sum=if(is_json=1, degraded_sum+tonumber(d3,10), degraded_sum)
| rex field=jraw max_match=0 "state_name\"\\s*:\\s*\"active\\+remapped\"\\s*,\\s*\"count\"\\s*:\\s*(?<d4>\\d+)"
| eval degraded_sum=if(is_json=1, degraded_sum+tonumber(d4,10), degraded_sum)
| eval degraded_pg_count=if(is_json=1, degraded_sum, null())
| rex field=jraw max_match=0 "(?<chk>\"(OSD_[A-Z0-9]+|PG_[A-Z0-9]+|MON_[A-Z0-9]+|MGR_[A-Z0-9]+)\"\\s*:\\s*\\{)"
| eval is_flap=if(is_json=0 AND match(_raw, "(?i)marked itself down|wrongly marked me down|calling new election|mon\\.[0-9]+ .{0,50}new election|(?<![A-Za-z])boot(?![A-Za-z])"),1,0)
| rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)osd\\.(?<osd_id>\\d+)"
| stats
    max(cluster_id) as cluster_id
    latest(health_status) as health_status
    latest(_time) as _evtime
    latest(num_osds) as num_osds
    latest(num_up_osds) as num_up_osds
    latest(num_in_osds) as num_in_osds
    latest(num_mons) as num_mons
    latest(quorum_count) as quorum_count
    max(degraded_pg_count) as degraded_pg_count
    max(total_pg) as total_pg_count
    sum(is_flap) as flap_hits
    values(osd_id) as flap_osd_ids
    values(chk) as active_health_checks
  by host
| join type=left max=0 host
    [search index=virtualization sourcetype="proxmox:ceph" earliest=-1h@m
     | spath
     | spath "monmap"
     | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "\"fsid\"\\s*:\\s*\"(?<fsid2>[^\"]+)\""
     | eval c=coalesce('monmap.fsid', fsid2, 'fsid')
     | sort 0 - _time
     | stats latest(c) as cluster_lookup by host
    ]
| fillnull value="" cluster_id
| fillnull value="" cluster_lookup
| eval cluster_id=if(cluster_id!="", cluster_id, cluster_lookup)
| eval cluster_id=if(cluster_id!="", cluster_id, "UNMAPPED-".host)
| fillnull value=0 flap_hits
| fillnull value=0 degraded_pg_count
| fillnull value=0 total_pg_count
| fillnull value=0 num_osds
| fillnull value=0 num_up_osds
| fillnull value=0 num_in_osds
| fillnull value=0 num_mons
| fillnull value=0 quorum_count
| eval osd_loss_pct=if(num_osds>0, round((num_osds-num_up_osds)/num_osds*100,1), 0.0)
| eval quorum_loss_pct=if(num_mons>0, round((num_mons-quorum_count)/num_mons*100,1), 0.0)
| eval pg_degraded_pct=if(total_pg_count>0, round(degraded_pg_count/total_pg_count*100,1), 0.0)
| eval max_flap_count_per_osd=flap_hits
| eval flap_score=case(flap_hits>=3, "high", flap_hits>=1, "low", 1=1, "none")
| eval recent_flap_osds=if(isnotnull(flap_osd_ids), mvjoin(flap_osd_ids, ", "), "none")
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    health_status="HEALTH_ERR" OR health_status="POLL_FAILED" OR quorum_loss_pct>0 OR osd_loss_pct>=25 OR pg_degraded_pct>=10, "critical",
    health_status="HEALTH_WARN" OR osd_loss_pct>=10 OR pg_degraded_pct>=5 OR flap_score="high" OR (num_in_osds>0 AND num_in_osds<num_up_osds), "warning",
    (flap_hits>0) OR (pg_degraded_pct>0) OR (num_in_osds>0 AND num_in_osds<num_up_osds), "watch",
    (health_status="HEALTH_OK" OR isnull(health_status)) AND flap_hits=0 AND pg_degraded_pct=0, "ok",
    1=1, "watch"
  )
| eval sev_order=case(severity_bucket="critical",1, severity_bucket="warning",2, severity_bucket="watch",3, 1=1,4)
| table cluster_id, health_status, num_mons, quorum_count, quorum_loss_pct, num_osds, num_up_osds, osd_loss_pct, total_pg_count, degraded_pg_count, pg_degraded_pct, recent_flap_osds, flap_score, active_health_checks, severity_bucket, sev_order
| stats
    values(health_status) as _hmul
    max(num_mons) as num_mons
    max(quorum_count) as quorum_count
    max(quorum_loss_pct) as quorum_loss_pct
    max(num_osds) as num_osds
    max(num_up_osds) as num_up_osds
    max(osd_loss_pct) as osd_loss_pct
    max(total_pg_count) as total_pg_count
    max(degraded_pg_count) as degraded_pg_count
    max(pg_degraded_pct) as pg_degraded_pct
    values(recent_flap_osds) as recent_flap_osds
    max(flap_score) as flap_score
    values(active_health_checks) as active_health_checks
    min(sev_order) as sev_order
  by cluster_id, severity_bucket
| eval health_status=if(mvfind(_hmul, "HEALTH_ERR")>=0, "HEALTH_ERR",
    if(mvfind(_hmul, "POLL_FAILED")>=0, "POLL_FAILED",
    if(mvfind(_hmul, "HEALTH_WARN")>=0, "HEALTH_WARN",
    if(mvfind(_hmul, "HEALTH_OK")>=0, "HEALTH_OK", mvindex(_hmul,0)))))
| fields - _hmul
| sort 0 sev_order, -osd_loss_pct
```

## Visualization

(1) **Single value** — worst `severity_bucket` in the picker window, split **optional** by `cluster_id` using a `stats` with `min(sev_order)`. (2) **Timechart** — `num_up_osds` and `quorum_count` (from JSON) by `cluster_id` with dual axis if needed. (3) **Table** — `cluster_id`, `health_status`, `osd_loss_pct`, `quorum_loss_pct`, `pg_degraded_pct`, `flap_score`, and **multi-value** `active_health_checks`. (4) **Sparkline** (clone) — `timechart` of flap keyword counts by `host` to spot **per-node** nervousness. (5) **Drill** — pre-built search from the row: `index=virtualization` filtered to the **same** `cluster_id` and time window, split by `sourcetype` across JSON vs syslog for evidence in one screen.

## Known False Positives

•

- **Planned disk or OSD replace** with intentional **out** → **in** and **reweight**; short windows of `HEALTH_WARN` from **degraded** PGs while **backfill** is normal when the change record exists.
•
- **Rapid PG autoscaler** or **balancer** activity can spike placement churn without customer-visible I/O pain — use `pg_degraded_pct` and `active+clean` share from a parallel panel before paging.
•
- **Scrub** or **deep-scrub** hitting an OSD that is **temporarily** slow: log lines that resemble flap but **no** `HEALTH_ERR` in JSON — require JSON-side confirmation.
•
- **MON_CLOCK_SKEW** during **NTP** migration or a **stratum** change — the fix is time sync, not disk.
•
- **RECENT_CRASH** with **empty** or **stale** crash archives after a one-time MGR or OSD assert — not a new hardware failure until `ceph crash` shows a pattern.
•
- **Single-site stretch** or **tiebreaker** topologies with **odd** but **expected** election chatter during a **witness** or **arbitration** test — this UC is a signal, not a substitute for a stretch runbook written by the cluster designer.
•
- **RGW-only** or **MDS** noise on a cluster that is **only** rbd in Proxmox — you may get **PG** health that does not block VM disk when RGW is the failing surface; confirm **client impact** in the UI before a storage bridge.
•
- **BlueStore** `BLUEFS` warnings as **soft** capacity pressure — the UC may rate **watch**; capacity still needs a JIRA, but it is not always an immediate I/O **failure** event.
•
- **Imfile** or **logrotate** races that **duplicate** a harmless **boot** line on restart — a single duplicate line should not drive `flap_score` to **high**; confirm **three or more** related lines per OSD per hour in raw search before a hardware RMA.
•
- **Lab** clusters with **synthetic** fault injection (ceph.io **vstart**-style) — `fsid` and `host` names will not match production CMDB; filter those with a `where cluster_id!="*lab*"` or a dedicated `index=lab` policy.

## References

- [Ceph Documentation — Monitoring OSDs and placement groups](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/rados/operations/monitoring-osd-pg/)
- [Proxmox VE Wiki — Hyper-converged Ceph cluster](https://pve.proxmox.com/wiki/Deploy_Hyper-Converged_Ceph_Cluster)
- [Ceph — Cluster health (`ceph -s -f json` structure and `health.checks`)](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/rados/operations/monitoring/)
